title: quercus infectoria subsp. boissieri
intro: Quercus infectoria subsp. boissieri, also known as the Gall Oak, is a subspecies of the Quercus infectoria plant, which is a deciduous tree native to the Mediterranean region. The Gall Oak has been traditionally used in medicine for its astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a valuable treatment for various health issues. The medicinal properties of Quercus infectoria subsp. boissieri have been found to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, treating digestive disorders, and alleviating skin conditions, as well as promoting wound healing and oral health. Herbal preparations of the Gall Oak include extracts, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and enjoy its numerous health benefits.
